summary: Verified selected Continue smoke paths on Raell Dottin's iPhone (00008130-000A090910C1401C, iOS 26.4.2) from a clean-tree build at commit c49e5adb01c48ca3eae5ab96aedd09ae06a29c30 on branch claude/trusting-jepsen-0acf20. Captured five device screenshots: Build Info gate, Today launch, and three Continue tap-routing paths (writing -> note detail sheet, training -> session card, home protocol -> active run sheet). Three source kinds (.homeProtocolRun, .trainingSession, .writingNote) and three routing paths reach device-verified for the captured configuration. Three other source kinds (.focusItem, .homeTask, .carriedFocusItem) and three swipe actions (Done, Defer, Drop) plus Add-to-Focus and homeTask routing remain not-attempted-yet because the device's natural data did not expose them; recorded honestly in the proof pack manifest rather than synthesized.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008130-000A090910C1401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-device-proof/DerivedData -allowProvisioningUpdates (clean tree at c49e5adb01c4)

xcrun devicectl device install app --device 00008130-000A090910C1401C /tmp/owlory-device-proof/DerivedData/Build/Products/Debug-iphoneos/Owlory.app (installed at /private/var/containers/Bundle/Application/92F8F245-7840-4283-9D93-604CEAC260FC/Owlory.app/)
On-device Build Info verified: Commit c49e5adb01c4, Branch claude/trusting-jepsen-0acf20, Tag c49e5ad, Built 2026-05-11T16:31:58Z, Configuration Debug, Bundle com.raelldottin.owlory, GitStatus clean.
On-device Today launch verified: dashboard renders with Day-in-progress hero, Check-in, populated Continue section, and downstream sections (screenshot 02).

On-device tap routing verified for three Continue source kinds: .writingNote -> Write note detail sheet (screenshot 03), .trainingSession -> Train session card (screenshot 04), .homeProtocolRun -> active run sheet (screenshot 05).
recommended_next_reason: Device-verified proof is now anchored for the captured surfaces. The next slice in the UI proof completion roadmap is testflight-proof, which must first pick one of three approaches (real data, TestFlight-safe fixture mode, or launch+Build-Info-only) because debug-only seed flags do not run in Release builds. Today launch + Build Info gate are the minimum honest TestFlight claim today.

contract_status_changes.before: Maintained XCUITest smoke covered all 6 Continue source kinds (visibility) plus 4 routing paths plus 3 Focus actions on the iPhone 16 simulator (`make ui-smoke`, 13 tests), and the simulator screenshot pack at `automation/proofs/owlory-ui-smoke-proof/` preserved 13 PNG artifacts with manifest hashes for that surface. No artifact existed for the same surfaces on a physical device. The slice's recommended_next_slice from the prior proof pack pointed at this slice; device-verified remained in missing_proof_levels for every Continue source and route.
contract_status_changes.after: The maintained smoke surfaces have a device-verified mirror at `automation/proofs/owlory-ui-device-proof/` covering Build Info gate, Today launch, and three Continue tap-routing paths (.writingNote -> Write note detail sheet, .trainingSession -> Train session card, .homeProtocolRun -> active run sheet) on Raell Dottin's iPhone (00008130-000A090910C1401C, iOS 26.4.2) at commit c49e5adb01c48ca3eae5ab96aedd09ae06a29c30 on branch claude/trusting-jepsen-0acf20. Build Info gate visually matches the stamped Info.plist; clean-tree stamping (worktree-aware) is confirmed end-to-end. The proof pack records device-verified for those three source kinds plus three routing paths and not-attempted-yet for `.focusItem`, `.homeTask`, `.carriedFocusItem`, Done/Defer/Drop swipe actions, `.homeTask` routing, and Add-to-Focus, with one-line reasons each (Focus Three empty, .home domain cap filled by protocol runs, no 3+ day Focus streak, no Focus-backed row on device, etc.).
proof: automation/proofs/owlory-ui-device-proof/02-today-launch.png (Today dashboard populated with real user data)

residual_risks: device-verified is claimed only for the three source kinds and three routing paths the device's live data could exercise honestly. Adding the deferred surfaces (.focusItem visibility, Done/Defer/Drop, .homeTask routing, .carriedFocusItem) requires the device to be in a different data state — either by user-driven setup before the next pass or by an intentional, narrow, TestFlight-safe fixture mode if the testflight-proof slice opens that door. Do not synthesize device data via undocumented private debug paths.

Build Info gate matched on first try this pass. If a future device pass shows -dirty, no-git, or a commit mismatch, the proof pack drifted; the recapture procedure in the README must be re-run from a clean tree. The handoff for that re-run should explicitly call out the drift cause (operator left worktree dirty, debug-only seed flag confusion, etc.).

The capture loop is manual: operator screenshots on device, AirDrops or saves to a /tmp staging directory, then the agent copies into the proof directory and regenerates the manifest. A future tooling slice could automate parts (xcrun simctl io equivalents do not exist for physical devices; Apple's `automation` framework or QuickTime Player screen recording are options) but each adds operational complexity. For now the manual loop is the documented recapture procedure.

Routing captures prove only the destination's first frame (the auto-presented sheet or the highlighted row immediately after tab switch). They do not exercise destination-internal interactions (note edit, run step completion, training reflection input). If a future product change moves the auto-present trigger or the highlight target type, these tests would silently still pass on the first frame even if the deeper destination behavior regressed; the simulator XCUITest suite is the durable guard for the assertion-level contract.

The .homeTask not-attempted-yet rationale depends on the current Continue admission policy (5 total / 2 per .home). If `home-protocol-schedule-stale-treatment` or any future schedule-driven projection slice changes which protocol runs are admitted, the device's Continue could expose a .homeTask row in a future pass; the README's not-attempted-yet table must be reviewed against admission rule changes.
